summary: Embedded-member fence speaks the kernel noun: recut genre.→member. both directions, emit gains the write face (blocks() renders embedded members), genre()/GenreValue/Locus-tag dissolve

edit.description: Recut the read fold to the kernel noun: `parse_embedded_info` strips `member.` not `genre.` (line ~558) so the fence info string is `member.<kind> <key>`, and recut the `genre fence`/`genre.<kind>`/`EmbeddedMember` doc vocabulary throughout (lines ~167-568).

Recut the fence-consuming primitive + nesting-template docs to `member.`: the child-kind fence info (`genre.<kind>`→`member.<kind>`, lines ~85-278) and the manifest-leaf fence (`toml genre.manifest`→`toml member.manifest`, lines ~511-512, and its two unit tests at ~994/1006).
Recut the render island's doc vocabulary (`genre fence`, `hard-coded genre`→member, lines 3/11); `render_member` already renders `EmbeddedMember` — behavior unchanged.
Recut `KindFactRow` doc vocabulary naming the host's nesting templates (`child/genre kind`→member, lines ~267/1133).

Implement the write face: `resolveBody`'s `blocks` branch (line ~53) renders each embedded member as a `member.<kind> <key>` TOML fenced block into the host body instead of throwing the `(genre-fence-format)` deferral; recut the `a genre member` comment (line ~133).
Recut `blocks()` and the `Blocks` prose type (`values`) to consume the re-homed embedded-member value shape (was `GenreValue[]`); recut the `(genre-fence-format)`/`genre value` doc vocabulary.

Recut the `Locus` union tag `"genre"`→`"embedded"` (line ~47); dissolve the `genre()` constructor (a kind with an embedded locus is built by `kind()`, lines ~158-172); re-home the embedded-member value shape (was genres.ts' `GenreValue`/`genreValue`) recut to the kernel noun; recut the `genre` doc vocabulary (lines ~42/159).
Recut the embedded-locus check `locus.kind === "genre"`→`"embedded"` (line ~200) and the genre-vocab doc comments (lines ~24/194/208-214/228/308).

Recut the barrel exports: drop the `genres.ts` re-exports (`GenreValue`/`genreValue`, lines ~20-22), export the re-homed embedded-member value from `kind.ts`; recut the `genre`/`genres` doc vocabulary (lines ~3/49/61).
Recut the 3 `genre` vocabulary refs to the kernel noun.
Recut the `genre.<kind> <key>` fixtures to `member.<kind> <key>` (10 refs) and add write↔fold round-trip coverage.
Recut the `genre.` fences (4 refs) to `member.`.
Recut the `genre` vocab ref; assert embedded members still emit no per-member declaration/provenance rows.
Recut `genre` fixture vocab; regenerate the four `display_rule__*` snapshots below via `cargo insta`.
Regenerate for the recut render vocabulary.
Regenerate for the recut render vocabulary.
Regenerate for the recut render vocabulary.
Regenerate for the recut render vocabulary.
Regenerate if the `genre`→member recut changes the rendered impact vocabulary.

Recut the `genre` fences/vocab (26 refs) and add write-face coverage: a `blocks()` body emits the `member.<kind> <key>` TOML fence, round-tripping through the engine fold to identical embedded members.

tests.asserts: a blocks() body projects the `member.<kind> <key>` TOML fence into the host body; the emitted fence round-trips through the engine fold to identical EmbeddedMembers
the fold reads `member.<kind> <key>` fences (not `genre.`) into typed EmbeddedMembers; a `genre.`-prefixed fence is now a plain block, not a nested member

acceptance: An SDK blocks() body emits `member.<kind> <key>` TOML fences the engine fold reads back byte-identically (write↔read round-trip); no `genre` noun survives in src/ or sdk/ (grep clean); `genre()` and genres.ts are gone and embedded members carry no lock rows — every placement speaks the kernel noun (representation.md, 'locus').
notes: 0012, atomic recut: read fold (parse_embedded_info genre.→member.) and SDK write face (emit.ts resolveBody blocks branch) coupled by the round-trip → one entry despite wide surface (~77 refs/13 files, 5 snapshots, emit.test.ts). GenreValue re-homes to an embedded-member value (name is code's; kernel noun=member). Embedded members already carry no provenance rows (isProjected=at) — not new work. scoped at 4cdd3b2.

summary: Ship the three acquisition channels — SDK npm package, engine binary pinned via per-platform optionalDependencies + standalone assets, the plugin/bundle — fail-loud on a missing platform binary

gate.reason: needs the per-platform engine-binary release workflow build-out + John's decide-at-release calls (optional Apple notarization for the standalone mac binary, USPTO name screen) before the mechanical wrappers validate end-to-end. No marketplace/signing creds exist — a marketplace is a git repo + marketplace.json, community submission a free web form gated by `claude plugin validate` (verified 2026-07-06, code.claude.com/docs); npm creds done 07-05. SDK scope resolved (`@dtmd/temper` 0.0.4, single package, `(sdk-package-layout)`); channel 1's SDK foundation shipped. No `.github/workflows/release.yml` exists (only the `temper.yml` check job); root package.json is still the private flume manifest.

new.description: Build the per-target release binaries and publish them to the npm platform packages (channel 2: the engine binary pinned by the SDK via per-platform `optionalDependencies`, plus standalone GitHub release assets), and produce/publish the plugin bundle with its `marketplace.json` (channel 3). Channel auto-detected. Distinct from any CI *check* job, which is a documented two-line user-authored job (`temper check` + `emit --frozen` byte-compare), not a generated file (distribution.md).

edit.description: The root `package.json` today is the *private* flume control-plane manifest (`temper-flume-harness`, `private: true`). The npm distribution entry needs platform-specific `optionalDependencies` pointing at per-target prebuilt-binary packages plus a launcher that resolves and execs the platform binary, erroring loudly if none is installed (fail-loud, distribution.md). Reconcile with the flume manifest rather than clobbering it. NB distinct from sdk/package.json (`@dtmd/temper`, already published — the engine pin is declared there, once).

acceptance: A stranger acquires temper via the SDK's pinned engine binary (per-platform optionalDependencies), standalone release assets, or the plugin/bundle (marketplace.json); a missing platform binary is an install error, never a silent skip (distribution.md 'Fail-loud invariant').
notes: Release infra outside src/; cargo gates can't validate npm/bundle publish; needs human creds. ch1 = SDK npm `@dtmd/temper`; ch2 = engine binary via per-platform optionalDependencies + standalone; ch3 = plugin/bundle via `temper bundle` + marketplace.json. Parked on human release creds + the engine-binary workflow. install.rs range-correctness shipped in SCAFFOLD-OUTPUT-VALID (^0.0.4); release owns only future re-pins.
